content.full_text:
Law Offices of

RICHARD P. SYBERT
2229 RIVER PLAZA DRIVE
SUITE 349
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95833
TEL/FAX: (916) 929-0962

June 3, 1991


Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am writing to request an FPPC opinion with regard to the
following facts:

I am the Director of the Office of Planning and Research (OPR),
and also the chair of the Governor's Council on Growth
Management (GMC).

The functions of OPR are to perform research for the Governor,
including analysis of legislation as may be requested; to liaise
for the Governor's Office with city and county governments; to
provide technical planning and permitting assistance to local
jurisdictions and third parties who request it; to act
mechanically as the state clearinghouse for distribution of
certain environmental review and other documents; to provide
computer and certain administrative services to the Governor's
Office; and to pass on requests from local jurisdictions for
extensions of general plans and housing elements. Other land use
planning responsibilities of OPR are spelled out in the
California Government Code.

Other than requests for extensions, OPR does not generally have
"line" authority. OPR does not make substantive policy
decisions, but operates rather in advisory role to the Governor,
of whose office it is part. The Director of OPR is also a member
of the Governor's senior staff, and may take part in formulating
recommendations to him.

The functions of GMC are to address growth and growth management
issues in California, and to make recommendations on same to the
Governor. Again, GMC has no line authority and is limited to an
advisory role.

I am also a licensed attorney in California, and have been since
1973. Since taking office in Sacramento in January of this year,
I have largely divested myself of my practice save for performing
advertising review and advice to The Perrier Group (Perrier),
which manufactures and sells a variety of bottled waters,
including Perrier, Arrowhead, and Calistoga. I review such
advertising and render advice on whether it complies with
applicable law in California and other Western states.

This image appears to be a mostly blank or very faded document page. The page is predominantly white/cream colored with minimal visible text or content. There are some very faint markings or creases visible, particularly along the edges, but no clearly legible text can be discerned from this image.

I have also discussed with a New York law firm ("the Firm") the possibility of associating with them on an "of counsel" or contract basis. The Firm consists of approximately twenty attorneys and its practice is exclusively intellectual property, including trademarks, copyrights, and patent law. I have had a correspondent counsel relationship with the Firm for over a decade. The proposal being discussed would be to retain me on a fixed-fee, contract basis as "of counsel," and to use my private law office as the Firm's California office. The Firm presently has no office outside of New York, although it has had a flow of matters in California, mostly federal court litigation. Under the proposal being discussed, I would have no direct interest in the profits of the Firm.

I request the FPPC's opinion on the following questions:

1. Is my work for Perrier or possible similar advisory work for other clients permitted, or permitted under certain conditions?

2. Is my proposed arrangement with the Firm permitted, or permitted under certain conditions?

Thank you for your assistance on these matters. I am available to provide any additional information requested. I appreciate your help.

Yours truly,

[signature]

Richard P. Sybert

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank or very faint document image. The image shows what looks like a cream or off-white colored page with minimal visible text or content. I cannot reliably make out any legible text to transcribe.

If there is text present that I'm unable to read due to image quality, faintness, or contrast issues, please either:
- Provide a clearer or higher contrast version of the image
- Confirm if this is intentionally a blank page
